metas.name: Behind Closed Doors
metas.description: A murder suspect on the run forces his way into Morgan's home and holds her family captive. She does everything she can to keep them safe, but it seems his arrival at her door may not have been so random after all.

metas.name: Pentatonic Scales
metas.description: P-Town Productions tackles nostalgia and the absurd in their culminating project.

metas.name: The Parking Spot
metas.description: A man’s life unravels after a parking spot is stolen from him.

metas.name: BatFreak Part II: Throbin for Justice
metas.description: With the Woker and their criminal minions on the rise, the dashing duo BatFreak and Throbin must find a way to keep the city streets safe- and they're gonna need some help!

metas.name: Short Films About Grief
metas.description: A series of vignette style short films that cover grief in many different ways.

metas.name: Something Old, Something New
metas.description: An ex-couple find themselves being pushed together by their friends at a wedding.

metas.name: billykid
metas.description: A photojournalist joins her childhood friend on a doomed heist for a Magic 8 Ball in their hometown of Hillsborough, North Carolina.

metas.name: Fingered!
metas.description: Disgruntled detective Janet Wolfe must interrogate her own wife Hannah Foxx over the murders of Janet's ex-wives. Things heat up as the couple's baggage begins to unravel while Janet attempts to get to the bottom of the case, even if that means destroying her marriage.

metas.name: Navigators of the Shadow Ring
metas.description: During the international conflicts of the 1950s, a modest crop dusting pilot searches for his heroic brother after he vanished at the hands of a masked villain obsessed with bringing the world's superpowers to their knees.

metas.name: Calm, serene
metas.description: A young woman and her grandmother talk about life

metas.name: Teacher
metas.description: A high school gym teacher deals with the loss of his mother and his sense of self as he clears out her old storage unit.

metas.name: Dose
metas.description: When Christopher is suddenly unable to pay for his medication, he is forced to learn to survive without control.

metas.name: The Audition
metas.description: During a self tape audition, a young man performs the version of himself he thinks the camera wants until small off screen moments reveal who he really is.

metas.name: One Friday Night
metas.description: Studious teen Emma is left home alone for the first time in a while, when she unexpectedly gets an invite to her first party, she embarks on a whirlwind of adventures meeting new people.

metas.name: Armani and the Birth of Italian Fashion
metas.description: In his first—and final—on-screen interview, Giorgio Armani reflects on a life that reshaped global fashion. Armani and the Birth of Italian Fashion traces the rise of Italy’s style empires through the legendary families who defined an era, from Armani and Versace to Gucci, Valentino, Prada, and beyond. Set within Milan’s most exclusive fashion sanctuaries, the film explores creative rivalries, personal stories, and the enduring romance between Hollywood and Italian design. Featuring rare insights from fashion icons, actors, and cultural tastemakers, this star-studded documentary is an intimate portrait of ambition, elegance, and the making of modern luxury.

metas.name: Bright Beautiful World: The Infectious Joy of Pat McCurdy
metas.description: Over more than 50 years and 12,000 performances in Milwaukee and beyond, Pat McCurdy has cultivated a fiercely loyal fan base and a reputation for spreading infectious joy. He may not be a household name, but to those who know him, Pat is unforgettable. BRIGHT BEAUTIFUL WORLD is an uplifting portrait of an artist who found success on his own terms—and of the deep, decades-long love affair between a musician and the fans who keep showing up.

metas.name: My Body Goes to Work
metas.description: A birth worker by day and a dancer in a strip club by night, Nevaeh navigates two very different worlds, in both of which bodies are judged and celebrated. As she reclaims the beauty and agency of her body while defying societal labels and expectations, she generously invites us to rethink our assumptions about sex, labour and healing.

metas.name: Aerobic Corridor
metas.description: Determined to complete the most extreme level of North America's oldest and longest cross-country ski event, three women traverse an unexpectedly serene and peaceful winter landscape on their two-day journey of exhilarating glides and challenging terrain, showcasing the impressive stamina and resolve required to reach the finish line.

metas.name: It Goes That Quick
metas.description: The word “personal” doesn’t begin to describe the profound intimacy of this unconventional family portrait, constructed with delicacy and humor by married filmmakers Ashley Connor and Joe Stankus. Neither can that description reveal the artful and intelligent self-awareness of their approach: It Goes That Quick is neither confessional nor exposé but rather something singular and rich—a timeless mosaic of American life, filmed over the course of eight years within one extended Northeastern network of aunts and uncles, parents and grandparents, adults and newborns in which everyone plays a dramatic role that’s no less constructed for being real. By the end of this fleet, loving feature, you feel like a member of the family.

metas.name: The Birth of Trap Music
metas.description: An exploration of how the architects who created the genre captivated the entire world, forever influenced the way music was made, and became a movement that gave the forgotten voices a platform to be heard.

metas.name: Gimme Truth
metas.description: False flags. Forbidden archeology. Chemtrails. Nefarious extraterrestrials. Fake pandemics. Through the intertwined stories of truth-seekers, influencers, and ex-believers, the film explores what draws people into the gravitational pull of conspiracy culture, and what happens when your truth becomes your identity.

metas.name: What is dyslexia?
metas.description: Before you read anything from searching the internet, discover the extraordinary potential of your dyslexic mind.

metas.name: White Nine
metas.description: White Nine is both a historical account of the independent living movement in Massachusetts and a compelling story of resilience, the power of collective action, and the enduring quest for equality, independence, and freedom for all. Fifty years ago, Joe, Christos, and Charlie, formed a profound bond as teens when their lives were forever changed by paralyzing spinal cord injuries-essentially a death sentence at that time. Their intertwined stories form the backbone of a larger narrative: the arduous fight for independence and civil rights for people with disabilities. Today, with enforcement weakening and legal challenges mounting, the ADA's promise of equality faces serious threats. As a country, we must actively defend these vital civil rights now to ensure people with disabilities are not pushed back into the margins. White Nine serves as a reminder of the battles fought, the progress achieved, and the critical work that still lies ahead.

metas.name: Mantis
metas.description: A grieving widow in denial takes a job using experimental tech that taps into residual brain activity after death.

metas.name: I'll Be Seeing You
metas.description: When a work errand derails Amy's weekend plans with her grandma, they embark on a road trip with one sentimental detour but need the help of a charming activities director.

metas.name: Surplus To Requirements
metas.description: A young research graduate survives a shuttle crash on a remote space station run by an experimental AI. With the crew missing, the AI keeps her occupied with routine maintenance, psychological tests, and endless games of chess. Slowly she realises the system is not evaluating her, but learning her and that she is about to be declared surplus to requirements…

metas.name: Confessions of a Modern Incel
metas.description: A young man begins to form negative views on women and relationships after several failed dating experiences.

metas.name: Brain Rot
metas.description: Benji needs his phone to fall asleep.

metas.name: Love Apptually
metas.description: Tired of swiping without results? Follow one journalist’s quest to unpack the dating app algorithms shaping modern romance as she reveals how, in an already lonely world, technology, bias and profit are increasingly influencing who we meet and why.

metas.name: Occupational Hazard: The First Coral Reefers
metas.description: Journey through the 1970s and early ’80s with the original members of Jimmy Buffett's Coral Reefer Band as they recount their contributions to iconic albums, life on tour, and the lasting impact they had in shaping a unique and enduring genre of music.

metas.name: Scenes From Suffolk
metas.description: Three stories of failure over the course of a single night.

metas.name: You Just Had to Be There
metas.description: A look at the community surrounding Nintendo's New York store in the month leading up to the launch of the Nintendo Switch in 2017.

metas.name: Squids
metas.description: After losing their beloved mentor days before the big national Scooter Race, the inexperienced Norah and her two best friends seek out the guidance of a reclusive idol to take down a wealthy, rival crew.

metas.name: Ancraophobia
metas.description: Shot on a Bolex set on a tripod. Each shot with the duration that the fully rewound motor of the Bolex runs. Then comes in a gust of wind.

metas.name: They Came Home
metas.description: A group of students try to find their missing friend. Meanwhile, a pair of teachers investigate a mysterious disease spreading throughout the school.

metas.name: STORM
metas.description: Short film accompanying the single "STORM" by GENER8ION & Yung Lean

metas.name: Five Minutes Straight of a Corner of a Room
metas.description: The year is 2016, and an experimental filmmaker decides to switch up her style, by speaking, in her first film with dialogue.

metas.name: Still You
metas.description: A senior in college is haunted by glimpses of her younger self

metas.name: Fabric
metas.description: The passage through life, nature and the fabric of time

metas.name: Cash Out!
metas.description: Facing a dangerous debt, a young man finds himself risking everything while his friend prepares to cross a line to protect him.

metas.name: A House Built on Lies
metas.description: Inspired by true events. When Daisy's husband is murdered, the news brings on a sudden, traumatic labor. Now, reliant on medication following a complicated birth, Daisy depends on her best friend Louise to help care for her son. But her husband’s death has triggered a strange series of events, and she soon finds herself in danger. Will she be able to uncover the truth in time to save herself and her new baby?

metas.name: Hijacked
metas.description: A rideshare abduction sparks a deadly race as a father hunts to rescue his influencer daughter and their loved ones from a deranged driver.

metas.name: Middlefield: The Goodman Paradox
metas.description: Follow the abstract journey of Sharon Goodman as she gets kidnapped by a mysterious entity nicknamed “the cave”.

metas.name: Lemon Limeade
metas.description: Lenny brings his friend Lyle to a local restaurant to experience THE drink of the season, Lemon Limeade.

metas.name: ephemeral
metas.description: A young man quietly prepares for an irreversible act before leaving his home and traveling toward the sea. Along the way, he encounters a woman whose presence feels both real and imagined. As memory and reality begin to blur, a fleeting connection interrupts his certainty.

metas.name: The Fellas
metas.description: Two young men must rekindle their friendship through the power of song after one of them starts spending too much time with their girlfriend.

metas.name: Pizza Angel
metas.description: Delivery in 20 minutes or less or the pizza's free! Should be easy right? Join a pizza delivery driver on his journey around town in search of the right address. Will he make it in time?

metas.name: In The Back Corner of a Coffee Shop Lies The Little Death
metas.description: An agent of the intelligence community must dispatch her French asset, who has become increasingly erratic. The task is made even harder when she realizes his true feelings for her.

metas.name: Fate
metas.description: Greg is a young gardener who lives a quiet, simple and lonely life. But that all changes when he bumps into a beautiful woman.

metas.name: Pursuit of Perfection - The Indy 500 with Conor Daly
metas.description: Pursuit of Perfection follows Conor Daly during the weekend of the Indianapolis 500, the most coveted prize in American open-wheel racing. As engines fire and pressure builds, Daly navigates the highs, lows, and unpredictable moments, offering an intimate look at what life is like for a driver chasing victory at the Greatest Spectacle in Racing.  Conor Daly is a native of Noblesville, Indiana, and much of the film was shot in and around the Indianapolis Motor Speedway.

metas.name: Licence
metas.description: A fruit vendor, denied a gun licence due to his low status, stages a fake attack with his friends to get around the system. But as the investigation deepens, it reveals a darker, deeply personal motive tied to his past.

metas.name: 2man From Hell
metas.description: An unsuspecting college kid gets dragged into a double date by his performative friend, only to find the girl he's paired with is an extraterrestrial.

metas.name: Lockdown
metas.description: During a sudden prison lockdown, a group of incarcerated men confront the emotional weight of confinement and discover that solidarity and human connection may be the only way through the darkness.

metas.name: Kill Your Darlings
metas.description: After cleaning out their apartment, Gwen and Austin realize that their relationship is not as perfect as they thought it was.

metas.name: Mona et ses voix
metas.description: As she celebrates her 40th birthday, Mona finds herself lost amid the voices crowding her head: the voice of society, the voice of the media, the voice of reason, the voice of the young girl she once was... A whimsical and feminist musical, *Mona and Her Voices* chronicles 24 hours in the life of a woman out of tune who is struggling to find her voice back.

metas.name: Dispatch
metas.description: A daughter grapples with dual inheritances from her birth family and marriage. The film examines the transmission of care, discipline, labor and faith through women’s daily work spanning different geographies and generations.

metas.name: Life Goes On
metas.description: BILL is in end of life care waiting for the sweet release of death - but unfortunately for him he is stuck in an infinite time loop, causing the day to restart the second his heart stops...  A darkly comic yet sweet and hopeful film all about enjoying the time you have left and saying the important things to the important people.

metas.name: Makin' Cake
metas.description: MAKIN' CAKE is a story of access and privilege in America told through the histories of baking and cake. Join multi-hyphenate artist and Milwaukeean, Dasha Kelly, as she travels the country talking to bakers, scientists, and historians, witness her nationally touring stage show of the same name, and maybe learn a few things about baking along the way. This doc is one part Food Network, one part history lesson, and 100% Dasha.

metas.name: Promotion
metas.description: When Daniel receives an opportunity to be promoted at the office, there’s only one thing he must do to prove his worth and reach the next step of his career... kill his closest colleague.

metas.name: Nekai Walks
metas.description: At 16, Nekai Foster was shot while walking home in Toronto's Jane and Finch neighbourhood. His journey of survival and recovery—defying all medical odds as he relearns to walk—exposes how gun violence shapes bodies, families and communities.

metas.name: The Last Days of April
metas.description: The courageous journey of a determined disabled advocate living with a tethered spinal cord and chronic pain, while balancing love, legacy and the fight for bodily autonomy as she chooses how she wants to leave the world: on her own terms.

metas.name: London's Last Wilderness
metas.description: When an alien visitor discovers it can communicate with Earth’s oceans, it becomes the only intermediary between humanity and a vast marine intelligence whose patience with the human race is running out.  Part survey, part discovery, the film explores a stretch of the wilderness that has largely slipped through the cracks of human attention, and in some places, bears the marks of human failure.  The alien encounters a force both beautiful and terrifying, a voice as old as the planet itself, carrying memories, warnings and a power beyond human control.  The film is a parable about nature on Earth by establishing an unidentified alien as interacting with the planet.  By way of subtle messages the sea warns the alien that the existence of the planet is at stake.  The alien discovers a controlling city but does not interfere with it and hopes that the sea will find a way to survive.

metas.name: Shower Thoughts
metas.description: A man takes a shower and is surprised to find out that all of his “shower thoughts,” normal and strange, have become a reality.

metas.name: The Astronaut is Unimpressed
metas.description: Young reporter Lewis Lapham is invited by Aldous Huxley to report on a trip he will never forget....

metas.name: Don’t Let It Bring You Down
metas.description: As his relationship and family structure falls apart, Ben struggles to connect with anyone in his life while spiraling into alcoholism and isolation.

metas.name: Maybe I
metas.description: After the end of a short relationship with her best friend, Clara, Sophie struggles to understand what went wrong and has to decide who to blame for all that happened.

metas.name: When Ocean Rises
metas.description: A polar bear, a penguin and a dog fight over a house, unaware that rising sea levels are burying everything - but they end up winning something far more valuable.

metas.name: Snake Island: The Volcanic Life of Sea Kraits
metas.description: On the reefs of Pulau Manuk in Indonesia’s Banda Arc, sea kraits thrive among coral rooted in a volcanic island rising from deep ocean. As their daily rhythms unfold, a rare behaviour emerges. Sea kraits hunt in groups alongside schools of bluefin trevally, forming one of the ocean’s most elusive predator alliances.

metas.name: Silver Rush
metas.description: Silver Rush is a thrilling 12-minute documentary that follows the incredible journey of salmon along Alaska’s Kenai River. The film chronicles the salmon’s perilous migration from the river’s mouth, where they face dipnetters to the Russian River, where they fight their way up waterfalls. Along the way, they encounter predators such as eagles and bears, as they work to reach their spawning grounds. Highlighting their critical role as a keystone species, Silver Rush underscores the essential connection between the salmon, Alaska’s ecosystems, and its people, illustrating how this species’ survival is vital to the region's balance and prosperity.

metas.name: HOMELESS
metas.description: HOMELESS is a documentary that humanizes unhoused people and explores their backgrounds, dreams and struggles to find the way home. The film aims to raise awareness and funds to end homelessness, which is an ever more urgent global challenge: The United Nations Human Settlements Program estimates that 1.6 billion people live in inadequate housing, and the best available data suggests that more than 100 million people have no housing at all.

metas.name: Wolf Cat Fever
metas.description: Furr Fever is the wild, fuzzed-up guitar-slinger of The Sextortions, a rockabilly band as deranged as they are irresistible. When Furr crosses paths with the howling, leather-clad Wolfy Howl, the two lost souls collide in a blaze of glitter and guitars in overdrive. But scheming music moguls, a corrupt Trump-ish mayor and a backstabbing bandmate are all gunning to tame them. Fortunately, the ghost of The King lives in Furr’s gold guitar, and rock and roll cannot be caged.

metas.name: Escape
metas.description: In the marshes of New South Wales, Australia, the green and golden bell frog faces an invisible enemy: the Chytrid fungus. This deadly pathogen has wiped out over 90 amphibian species, and the frog's survival depends on an unlikely cure - a frog sauna. As the fungus attacks, weakening the frog's body, time is running out. While predators lurk in the sky and grasses, the true threat lies beneath the water. In a desperate search for safety, the frog finds refuge in a sauna that may hold the key to survival. But will this fragile hope be enough to save the frog? This family-friendly short film uses elements of horror and whimsy to tell the story in a new and captivating light.

metas.name: The Detectorists
metas.description: The Detectorists is a coming-of-age story about Cariad, a young detection dog learning to use her powerful nose to help protect wildlife. Set on the riverbanks of rural Wales, Cariad teams up with her owner Lee and seasoned sniffer dog Neo on a mission to uncover signs of otters—an elusive, protected species. But learning to read the land isn’t easy, and Cariad has a lot to prove. With every scent trail and muddy pawprint, she edges closer to becoming a vital part of the conservation team. It’s a story of instinct, training, and finding your place—one sniff at a time.

metas.name: Purple Emperor
metas.description: Butterflies and Music, The male purple emperor possesses the iridescence that gives it its name: the metallic purple shimmer is a structural colour created by light refraction through special scales. Its scientific name (apatura iris) also reflects this: Iris is the goddess of the rainbow. The butterfly is often seen feeding on faeces, carrion, or even human sweat, as it primarily obtains nutrients from minerals

metas.name: Hope
metas.description: On the last day of college, three lives quietly collide with memories, choices, and unspoken feelings. Hope is a poetic reflection on timing, love, and the moments that could have changed everything.  Hope follows three hearts bound by silence, longing, and what remains unsaid. A gentle story about love, timing, and the fragile beauty of unanswered emotions.

metas.name: Nikki Glaser: Good Girl
metas.description: Considered one of the most fearless voices in modern comedy, Nikki Glaser delivers a brutally honest special that takes on aging, beauty, and fame - confronting what it means to stay relevant, desirable, and real in a culture that never looks away.

metas.name: Pavements
metas.description: Finlay and Joe are processing the tragic death of their friend, Luke. But when Finlay's unique coping mechanism becomes too problematic to ignore, a long-avoided conflict forces a confrontation that will change everything.

metas.name: Samanta Nobody
metas.description: A 16-year-old homeless girl on the streets of Sligo town. For Samanta, reaching out for help has only ever ended in closed doors, but she has a hidden gift for songwriting and performance. When a music shop owner spots her talent, she has a chance to finally accept that music could be the open door she’s been waiting for.

metas.name: 5D
metas.description: A grieving woman reflects on the toxic relationship she had with her recently deceased boyfriend.

metas.name: Original Sound
metas.description: A down on his luck aspiring musician gets his big break at the big time when an up and coming singer agrees to collaborate with him after she is caught stealing his song.

metas.name: August
metas.description: In their last summer together, two young men must confront the doom of change and come to terms with feelings they didn't know they had.

metas.name: Apex
metas.description: A grieving woman pushing her limits on a solo adventure in the Australian wild is ensnared in a twisted game with a cunning killer who thinks she's prey.

metas.name: Desert Warrior
metas.description: Set in seventh-century Arabia, Princess Hind (Aiysha Hart) defies her fate, refusing to become a concubine to the ruthless Emperor Kisra (Sir Ben Kingsley). Fleeing into the desert with her father, she is hunted by a merciless army and forced to trust a legendary bandit (Anthony Mackie) with secrets of his own. Rising from fugitive to fearless warrior, Hind unites warring tribes for a final stand - the Battle of Dhi Qar, a clash that will change history forever.

metas.name: Lincoln in the Bardo
metas.description: A short adaptation of the scene from the novel

metas.name: Pretty Guardian Sailor Moon the Miracle: Moon Palace Chapter Deluxe
metas.description: Experience the Moon Palace coming to life in Pretty Guardian Sailor Moon the Miracle: Moon Palace Chapter Deluxe, a spectacular show where a celestial ball erupts into a thrilling battle to protect the Legendary Silver Crystal. Watch Super Sailor Moon and the Sailor Guardians shine in a dazzling celebration of love, unity, and justice as they fight to restore hope and brilliance to the universe.

metas.name: Josh Jones - Lad On Tour
metas.description: After his 2023/24 sell-out smash tour Gobsmacked, this marvellous Manc is back with his best show yet. A TV favourite, and now an aspiring doctor’s wife, Josh Jones is now knee deep into his 30’s and nothing thrills him more than a Greggs Sausage Roll and an M&S food shop. Living a more sedate life isn’t without its challenges though – he’s still trying to earn his cat’s respect!  Josh is hitting the road with a show packed full of laughs and he’s keeping it light – nothing super political, nothing controversial and it’s definitely not going to change your life, but you can be sure of a fab night out filled to the brim with jokes about history, cats, his love of wrestling and much more.

metas.name: Indifference
metas.description: A lost up and coming guitarist looses the ability of his hands through a dark mental breakdown and addiction.

metas.name: The Cost of the Crown
metas.description: With his kingdom in tatters and his subjects baying for his blood, the king hides in his throne room praying for a last-minute reprieve. But with the mob at the door, who can he trust? His spoiled wife expects him to murder anybody who would do him harm, while his chief advisor plots from the shadows. Powerless, naive, and scared, will the king live long enough to learn the true cost of his crown? The Cost of the Crown is a historical drama about betrayal, class, and the inevitable corruption that ultimate power brings.

metas.name: Garage Band
metas.description: A group of young adults attempt to lowball their way into buying a priceless guitar for cheap with the end goal of starting a band. Chaos ensues.

metas.name: Danseur Dérangé
metas.description: As a jaded cabaret performer begins her routine, the world around her collapses.

metas.name: And Again I Dream
metas.description: Black Lesbian journalist and organizer Ivy Young knows that mainstream society will not preserve her story after she's gone, so she rallies friends and her niece Jade to defy the past for the sake of a righteous future.

metas.name: Poor Mr Fischer
metas.description: A man tries to catch a really big fish.

metas.name: BACKWARDS
metas.description: In the break of a punctual smoker who kill himself, kind of...

metas.name: AMADI
metas.description: Amadi is a young, college entrepreneur on a mission to host the event of a lifetime. With the help of her friends, they seek to find the right financier to support them until the money becomes the least of their worries.

metas.name: Squatters' Rights
metas.description: The relationship between a Frog and a Lumberjack intensifies as they argue over who has the rights to the river.

metas.name: Talking To Strangers
metas.description: A desperate man breaks into a stranger’s apartment, only to find himself trapped in a looping night where Death forces him to confront the cost of his choices.

metas.name: The SD Swap
metas.description: Introvert student, James, gets the wrong SD card for his final project. He has to get over his fears and talk to another student to get the right SD card. What will he do?

metas.name: A Little Death
metas.description: A gentleman happens upon an enchanting lady a top a bridge. She beckons him to come up and meet her. They exchange in a flirtatious dialogue of action. She lures him to the other side of the bridge and disappears. they will soon meet again... under the bridge.

metas.name: Monkey Paw
metas.description: On the last day of summer, two wrestlers face each other one final time.

metas.name: Check-In
metas.description: Two friends visit their recently laid-off pal after losing contact, only to find him acting odd and making some questionable choices.

metas.name: Guess Who's Back
metas.description: A couple grieve the sudden loss of their late friend, until he reappears at their doorstep.

metas.name: One Last Cheer
metas.description: During their last game as teammates, eight seniors confront the memories and emotions of a season—and a childhood—coming to an end.

metas.name: Reefs: Our Sustenance Beneath the Waves
metas.description: In 2024 El Salvador declared a marine sanctuary at the richly biodiverse and beautiful, Maculís Reef. This vibrant ecosystem is vital not only for its extraordinary marine life, including soft corals, oysters, lobsters, nurse sharks, and endangered species like hawksbill turtles, but also for the local communities that rely on its resources.  The protection of Maculís Reef strengthens the conversation of one of the El Salvador's richest coastal-marine ecosystems and affirms the country's world-leading reputation in environmental protection and sustainable development.  This visually stunning film celebrates the Reef and its protection and raises awareness of the invaluable and irreplaceable ecosystems found beneath the waves of El Salvador's shores.

metas.name: Value
metas.description: An intimate animated portrait of one individual's journey through anxiety and isolation, unfolding into a quiet, hard-earned sense of self-acceptance.

metas.name: Ghost Town
metas.description: In a Japanese coastal town haunted by the 2011 tsunami, the filmmaker installs her camera inside the city's taxis to capture conversations between the living and, it turns out, the dead.

metas.name: AntiDiva: The Carole Pope Confessions
metas.description: Carole Pope shattered taboos with her raw, unapologetic music and defiant sexuality—AntiDiva is the story of a queer rock icon reclaiming her place in the spotlight.

metas.name: Stars on the Ceiling
metas.description: On the war's anniversary, a Ukrainian woman in Toronto must choose between her fiancé's family or supporting her war-displaced niece.

metas.name: The Coronation
metas.description: Two of Hollywood’s loneliest creatures form an unlikely bond.

metas.name: Hysterile
metas.description: A perfectionist takes an exam whilst being tested by her surroundings. Will she overcome these challenges, or crumble under the pressure?

metas.name: Sgt. Haane
metas.description: This documentary-drama film follows Lance Sgt. Haane Manahi DCM of B Company, 28th Māori Battalion, a soldier of extraordinary courage, whose actions proved pivotal in the 1943 battle for the fortified summit of Takrouna, Tunisia.

metas.name: De let'en! - Da it'en!
metas.description: A meditative depiction of a colonial villa in Mungo, Surinam, taken back by nature.

metas.name: Hey! That's My Hat!
metas.description: After losing his hat, a man goes to great lengths to get it back.

metas.name: The World Of Bootlegs
metas.description: A documentary that delves into the intricate workings of the illegal bootlegging movie industry.

metas.name: Something Pointless
metas.description: When an unhappy young boy discovers an older man quietly affixing painted eggshells to a disused wall in their neighbourhood, he begins to understand how even the smallest, strangest acts can leave behind something lasting.

metas.name: Alphabet Lane
metas.description: An isolated couple lose control of a joke about imaginary friends.

metas.name: The Thing Expanded
metas.description: The ultimate companion to John Carpenter’s "The Thing", digging deep into the proverbial iceberg to enhance your viewing experience with new insights, stories, and revelations.

metas.name: Grandpa's Skins
metas.description: "Grandpa's Skins" is a short film that tells the story of Gabo, a 6-year-old boy, and his grandfather Ivan, who is in a state of dementia. With the tradition of the Kukers - where men wear huge leather suits to ward off evil forces - Gabo tries to bring back the memories of his grandfather and their relationship.

metas.name: Diena Kaune
metas.description: A day in interwar period Kaunas plays out with all sorts of adventures.

metas.name: Ed Night: Hour Long Clip
metas.description: Underrated stand up and asthmatic wretch Ed Night has been hearing a lot about how you need to be uploading stand up online recently. Well, here it is: the spectre at the feast bursts onto the big screen with a no-holds-barred hour of wailing and moaning in his debut special. Prepare to have your ribs tickled to the max by a deluge of comedy material about all sorts of different topics and subjects. In this must-see 49 minute long hour the two-time Edinburgh Comedy Award nominee (it’s not a science) muses on submarine malfunctions, meditates on having his pets destroyed by the government, and artfully navigates the thorny issue of self-checkout machines with heart as well as humour. Of course it isn’t perfect, but what is?

metas.name: Towels
metas.description: Louis loses his backpack in Barcelona and finds help from two guys who offer him a place to sleep. In this unfamiliar environment, he struggles with the intimacy of his hosts and finds himself somewhere between feeling overwhelmed and curious. The film explores what interpersonal closeness between men can look like and how we interpret it.

metas.name: Anti Star Tina Aumont
metas.description: A work-in-progress portrait of Tina Aumont by her longtime friend, artist and filmmaker Ivan Galietti. Through private moments, never-before-seen interviews, and film clips, this homage attempts to reveal the human being beyond the media clichés.

metas.name: Itam: A Sun Bear Story
metas.description: Three years in the making, this remarkable documentary was filmed entirely in Sabah, Malaysia. It follows the rehabilitation and release of a rescued sun bear named Itam. Developed in close collaboration with the Bornean Sun Bear Conservation Centre, the production was granted rare access to archival records, veterinary logs, field operations, and the complete rewilding process. This access allows the film to move beyond surface observation into deeply-researched, character-driven storytelling. Combining immersive cinematic language with investigative scientific inquiry, ITAM: A SUN BEAR STORY positions Southeast Asian conservation stories within global discourse.

metas.name: The real Matryoshka: A Documentary by Karime Pindter
metas.description: After more than a decade of showing herself without filters in front of millions of viewers, Karime Pindter shares how she is preparing for the most intimidating challenge of her life: performing at the finale of La Más Draga in front of 20,000 people at one of the country’s most iconic venues… without knowing how to dance.

metas.name: Bless
metas.description: A dog enters a life almost by chance… and quietly becomes everything.  Through four moments, this short film tells the story of a bond that feels simple — but means everything.  This is not just a story about a dog.  It’s a story about time… love… and letting go.  BLESS is a short film written and directed by Yann Mazieres, starring Ulysse — a dog actor whose presence carries the entire story.  “For you, it was part of your life. For him, it was all of it.”

metas.name: Trip and Chadwick: Code Pink
metas.description: Trip and Chadwick, two childhood best friends, are forced to embark on an epic journey to return their stolen final project before graduation.

metas.name: 3 Words
metas.description: What would you say if the world were ending in a few hours? As an asteroid speeds toward Earth, three high school students race against time to confess the truths they've long buried - a girl ready to risk her heart, a son afraid to reveal who he is, and a boy hiding a life-altering secret. But as emotions collide and relationships unravel, their "last words" may change everything - if they're brave enough to say them before time runs out.

metas.name: W.A.B. - A Memorial
metas.description: A short film reflecting on the life and final act of Wynn Alan Bruce, who died on April 22, 2022 in Washington, D.C.

metas.name: Golden Mirror
metas.description: In the brutal finale of an extreme reality show, drag queen Couenne and influencer Colette face off after weeks of public humiliation. As their vulnerability is exploited for ratings, the film questions the true cost of victory in a complicated media landscape.

metas.name: Sleight of Hand
metas.description: A part-time drug dealer breaks into their distributor's home to steal money for an important trip.

metas.name: The Deceptively Simple Need for a Home (on Other People’s Land)
metas.description: A woman revisits her 14 childhood homes as one might reread a personal diary. The camera, with its static grace, allows the memories to flow. A wall leaks, a tree becomes a friend, a body changes, Diana dies, a mother struggles. Between anecdotes and pivotal events, a political consciousness takes shape: for these homes are built on Aboriginal land.

metas.name: Singing of the Evening Stars
metas.description: Observed by an otherworldly gaze, the modern commercial downtown Los Angeles, immersed in the deepest night, becomes the setting for a sound ritual and visual symphony. Thanks to one man’s dreams, the songs of the many species of birds that once inhabited the area return – back from the time when a river still flowed through it and a native village stood there.

metas.name: Miss Lavender Supreme
metas.description: On the night of her homecoming dance, seventeen-year-old Chloe Salle, an undefeated small town beauty queen, finally succumbs to the weight of constant performance.

metas.name: Finding Satoshi
metas.description: At the center of Bitcoin lies the greatest unsolved mystery of the digital age. Who created it, and who is Satoshi Nakamoto? Finding Satoshi presents the conclusion reached after a four-year, evidence-based investigation, drawing on original reporting and rare access to key figures in crypto to uncover the human ideas, motivations, and philosophy behind the code that reshaped global finance.

metas.name: Orangutan
metas.description: Narrated by Josh Gad, journey high into the rainforest canopy of Southeast Asia where a magnificent and mysterious community filled with playful personalities lives. Witness the exhilarating action, heartwarming encounters, and a closeup look at a stunning jungle teeming with wildlife as the inquisitive adolescent orangutan Indah prepares to leave her family and venture out on her own for the first time.

metas.name: The Charger
metas.description: When a teacher leaves two students alone, a simple fight over a charger escalates into a full-blown battle.

metas.name: ATLANTIC CITY
metas.description: A young man in crippling debt is willing to do whatever it takes to secure a future for his family.

metas.name: Abraxas
metas.description: Struggling to complete his college thesis, a frustrated environmentalist starts to receive “divine inspiration” which liberates him from writer's block. However, as his ideas intensify, he spirals into a dangerous fixation, crafting an enraged manifesto against techno-industrial civilization.

metas.name: Asteria
metas.description: Jeremy and Tom Fidgerald are two conspiracy-minded cousins ​​and beekeepers who believe the company they work for is a front for aliens who are exploiting Earth's resources. Jeremy and his cousin take action on climate change, kidnapping the company's CEO to force her to admit she's an alien and establish contact with their mothership. The short film addresses the exploitation of resources by large corporations and encourages people to take climate action, even though it's not always possible.

metas.name: Red Wolf in Time Out
metas.description: The Red Wolf was tattled on for something it didn't do and now it's in time-out.

metas.name: Operation Wolf Foster
metas.description: Once nearly extinct, the critically endangered Mexican wolf is making a comeback - thanks to a bold conservation effort that places captive-born pups into wild dens. Operation Wolf Foster takes you inside this high-stakes recovery program, following the delicate journey of newborn wolves as they’re flown across the country and carefully introduced into the wild, offering hope for the species’ future.

metas.name: A Clawsome Tale
metas.description: This whimsical, entertaining short documentary dives into the wondrous world of The National Lobster Hatchery in Cornwall (UK). The National Lobster Hatchery opened in 2000, to help prevent a collapse in European Lobster populations in the region, following similar collapses in Scandinavia and the Mediterranean. Narrated by Lowenna (a European Lobster), the film follows her journey through the hatchery, and that of here offspring, who start their lives there before being released back into the ocean. Through her eyes, we also meet the passionate people working to protect her species: including Cornish fisherman Dan Gilbert and Head of Hatchery Production Ben Marshall.

metas.name: Stumble Then Rise On Some Awkward Morning
metas.description: A man embarks on a journey to meet his maker and finds nothing of any consequence.

metas.name: Tracks
metas.description: Jess plans to take a train ride, but things take a turn for the worse.

metas.name: Lainey Wilson: Keepin' Country Cool
metas.description: Lainey Wilson redefines what it means to be a modern country star while exploring her struggles and triumphs at a pivotal career moment.

metas.name: A Fire There
metas.description: In an isolated village in the high plains of southern Georgia, three lifelong friends from the Armenian community discuss their future.

metas.name: American Youngboy
metas.description: A concert film that explores both the scale of YoungBoy’s cultural impact and the personal realities of life on the road at the highest level.

metas.name: Unwired
metas.description: Outdated sheep fences have excluded pronghorn from prime habitat in the Red Desert of Wyoming for over 75 years. After record snowfall in 2022-23 caused thousands of animals to perish along the fences, a group of wildlife professionals discovered an area of more than 100,000 acres that was excluding pronghorn. Within a year's time, conservation groups and a rancher worked together to restore access to the pronghorn's historic habitats and give this species a better chance at surviving an uncertain future.

metas.name: The Birdman of Skomer
metas.description: For over 50 years, Tim Birkhead has dedicated his life to studying a resilient bird colony on the brink of survival. Once nearly wiped out, the birds have fought their way back - but new threats now loom on the horizon. As Tim grapples with their uncertain future, he also faces a deeper question: what will become of his life’s work?

metas.name: Missed Connection
metas.description: To spare her grieving mother, a young woman ventures across the border to  collect her grandmother's ashes, embarking on a journey that unearths long-buried childhood memories, and forces her to confront the bonds she never  got to form.

metas.name: The Shovelers
metas.description: A snow shoveling job goes wrong when a crazy client refuses to pay.

metas.name: Meat Grinder
metas.description: When Mac finds out his vegan girlfriend is cheating on him, he schemes a revenge plot that will change her attitude.

metas.name: Homerun
metas.description: A photographer on the verge of getting fired is given one last chance to shoot for an out of control pop star, Danny Hart.

metas.name: What The River Knows
metas.description: For the past several years, a lost Eden drowned under Lake Powell has been re-emerging, revealing the follies of the past and a new way forward for the Colorado River. What The River Knows is a film that explores this unique inflection point in a centuries-long history. At the same time that we are met with the need to redesign our systems of water management in the West, we are presented with the opportunity to restore one of the most stunning landscapes on the planet.

metas.name: Fervour
metas.description: A first date stirs memories for a woman who once participated in a dangerous direct action.

metas.name: Nothing to Lose
metas.description: When a terminally ill man returns home to find a masked robber stealing his late wife’s most cherished possession, an unexpected dinner turns into a high-stakes mind game with survival, violence, and revenge.

metas.name: Hotel Excelsior
metas.description: A mysterious concierge guides us through stories that unfold in the same room over a century. A Jewish businessman celebrates a lonely family festivity with his daughter. A planned suicide leads to a life-changing encounter. An ageing rock band struggles with their image, and a young woman sees a callboy as her big chance for financial independence.

metas.name: H5N1: Conservation in Crisis
metas.description: H5N1: Conservation in Crisis’ reveals the devastating impact of avian flu —not only on wildlife but on those fighting to protect it. David Steel, manager of the Isle of May, bears witness to the unfolding catastrophe, balancing conservation efforts with the emotional toll of loss. As avian flu tightens its grip, the island’s thriving bird colonies face yet another year with the virus outbreak, spreading rapidly through dense nesting grounds.The virus continues to spread and evolve, leaving one question: what does the future hold for seabirds—and for us?

metas.name: Memory Project
metas.description: When volunteer subjects participate in a simple Yale University experiment in 1962, the unsettling exercise uncovers disturbing truths deeply embedded in each human being.

metas.name: Humboldt USA
metas.description: Countless places across the United States still bear the name of Alexander von Humboldt — queer traveler, visionary naturalist, now largely forgotten. The longing filmmaker uses three of them as unlikely common ground, weaving Humboldt's own words through present-day lives: urban activists greening neglected neighborhoods, scientists scanning redwood forests, hunters returning bighorn sheep to protected land. Across generations and landscapes, HUMBOLDT USA asks what remains of a vision of "interconnectedness."

metas.name: Nobody Here: The Story of Vaporwave
metas.description: ‘Nobody Here’ traces the story of Vaporwave and its evolution into the internet’s most aesthetic and celebrated micro-genre — from its origins in obscure online communities to its emergence in the mainstream consciousness.

metas.name: Tikray
metas.description: Set in the twenty-first century, Tikray follows the story of a 17-year-old boy who moved from Peru to Geneva at the age of nine, tracing his struggle to preserve his cultural identity while navigating the challenges of growing up between two worlds.

metas.name: Something Real
metas.description: What drives us to keep going in a world that grows increasingly hostile toward our existence every day? Squirm Gallery, a DIY music venue, finds a way forward in a rapidly gentrifying Denver.

metas.name: Like Him
metas.description: A non for profit student made short film following the struggle of a young man grappling with the fear that he resembles his absent father. Focusing on the struggle to understand one's own identity in the face of perceived negative traits inherited from a parent. Follows the narrative of Tyler the creator's song 'Like Him.'

metas.name: Millie shoots and kills a guy (just for fun)
metas.description: the evil mastermind "millie" claims another victim in this sick and evil decent into madness from critically acclaimed director of "blood phallus" and "the evil tomato throwing bastard"

metas.name: The One That Got Away (Director's Cut)
metas.description: Unvaulted after 15 years. This extended version of the bittersweet ballad follows an elderly Katy looking back on a past, passionate relationship that fell apart due to a regretful dispute.

metas.name: The Burbank Beetleboy
metas.description: Screw Sasquatch. This CA city deserves its own cryptid — and one guy is determined to make it happen.

metas.name: The Chat
metas.description: A man returns home to cook dinner and discovers something sinister about the A.I. chatbot that assists him.

metas.name: The Devil to Pay
metas.description: Lilith has one final message for her adoptive father.

metas.name: Love is
metas.description: TRIANGLE - 1st Single Album ‘Love is’ M/V is out now! A music video following the comeback of popular trio group TRIANGLE after being forgotten by the public due to a scandal 20 years ago. MOVIE RELEASE 2026.06.03. The legend is back! TRIANGLE is Back!

metas.name: Casting Call
metas.description: Three girls find themselves fielding all sorts of personalities on dating apps in this absurdist comedy.

metas.name: Bangkok 24/7 - Italian Eyes
metas.description: This short film captures the vibrant moments, places, and emotions experienced and recorded over 24 hours in Bangkok, as seen through the eyes of talented Italian directors, producers, and actors who were invited to visit Thailand’s capital. Each of them documented unexpected experiences of the city at different times of the day and night. The result is an honest and unpredictable visual diary that reveals surprising perspectives of Bangkok.

metas.name: Enchanté
metas.description: When a desperate mark offers them a big reward, a weary grifter duo has to put their minds together to find his dog and pay their rent.

metas.name: As Far as We Imagine
metas.description: Hovering between the real and the imagined, a filmmaker traces their fragmented sense of place. Moving through maps, projections, digital simulations, they explore how ways of seeing shape how we belong. A search for position that is never fixed, but always felt in relation.  A journey and meditation on placelessness, image-making, perspective and the quiet politics of how we relate to our surroundings.

metas.name: The Dinner
metas.description: Mark has second thoughts about proposing to his girlfriend of six years, after a crisis strikes during an important dinner.

metas.name: The Interpreter
metas.description: The Interpreter follows Amjad, a Syrian refugee in the UK, as he retrains to become a community interpreter, returning to the very system that once interrogated him. Revisiting his own asylum interviews, Amjad navigates the charged dynamics between interpreter, displaced and authority. Through staged re-enactments, the film interrogates the ethics of neutrality and the interpreter’s role as both voice and barrier, where translation becomes an act of subversion and connection.

metas.name: The Crossover Episode
metas.description: When timid young Jackie is left in charge of her frail Grandma, she escapes into her favorite sitcom until a sudden health scare forces her to step into adult responsibility for the first time.

metas.name: The I.S.P
metas.description: A socially incompetent office worker is placed under the supervision of an AI employee programmed to help him achieve his first sale.

metas.name: The Rattening
metas.description: An everyday office worker is suddenly moved to a remote role, only to uncover a pest from work living in his walls.

metas.name: Untold: The Shooting at Hawthorne Hill
metas.description: A retired Olympic equestrian takes on a new dressage student at his idyllic farm in New Jersey. Over time, tensions mount between the two, leading to 911 calls, cryptic social media posts, and accusations of spying. The feud culminates in a shocking act of violence and a trial that rocked the prestigious and sophisticated world of dressage.

metas.name: Wild Hope: Building for Birds
metas.description: Millions of birds pass through our yards during their bi-annual migrations, but they face an invisible killer along the way: glass windows. In the US alone, an estimated one billion birds die each year when they collide with windows. Now, two organizations help pave the way to a bird-friendly future.

metas.name: The Rewilders
metas.description: The western world has long equated survival with conquering the wild.  But now more than ever, humanity’s survival depends upon co-existence with the wild.  Since 1970, the planet has lost 60% of its biodiversity. The most dire consequences are accelerated global warming and a potential mass extinction event affecting millions of plant and animal species. But there is a nascent solution gaining ground: rewilding the planet. Exploring the restoration of iconic species like jaguar, elephant, bison and Pacific salmon, The Rewilders is an intimate, visually spectacular short documentary that tells the tale of pioneering conservationists on three continents, working tirelessly as they galvanize the global movement of rewilding, one animal and landscape at a time.

metas.name: Aldeas, the Final Dream of Pope Francis
metas.description: Pope Francis's final in-depth interviews before his passing and follows Scholas Occurrentes' cinema initiative that brings together education, film production, and community building.

metas.name: JAUNT
metas.description: After a chance encounter in LA, Paul, a washed-up, bipolar photographer and Madeline, a free-spirited Parisian, embark on a road trip to San Francisco. As secrets surface and feelings grow, their impulsive getaway turns into a deeper journey neither of them expected.

metas.name: Dystopias Achieved
metas.description: Human beings have always maintained a complex pulse with nature and technology. Depending on the place that both have occupied throughout history, they have been fundamental in building our societies. Distopias Alcanzadas proposes a journey through five contemporary spaces that raise this difficult relationship. In them, technology is gradually occupying previously unknown and highly transformative terrain.

metas.name: Michael
metas.description: Discover the story of Michael Jackson, one of the most influential artists the world has ever known, and his life beyond the music, tracing his journey from the discovery of his extraordinary talent as the lead of the Jackson Five, to the visionary artist whose creative ambition fueled a relentless pursuit to become the biggest entertainer in the world, highlighting both his life off-stage and some of the most iconic performances from his early solo career.

metas.name: Stuck in the Middle
metas.description: 'Stuck in the Middle' is a nostalgic short dance film set in the early 1970s. It follows the lively journey of four young girls confined to their home during a record-breaking heat wave.

metas.name: Coyote Crossing
metas.description: Coyotes have survived in Southern California for over a million years and still navigate its neighborhoods today. Through science, indigenous knowledge, and firsthand accounts, Coyote Crossing explores how an ancient predator adapts across cities, suburbs, and the countryside. As fear and public policy collide, communities must decide what level of coexistence is possible — and what it will cost.

metas.name: Pressure Point
metas.description: Two collegiate-level athletes share their stories about falling in love with their sport and the prices they had to pay to get here today

metas.name: Unraveling the Dream
metas.description: In The Doors of Perception (1954), Aldous Huxley wrote that the urge to transcend self-consciousness was "a principle appetite of the soul." He hoped that psychedelics would make the experience of self-transcendence more widely available, and thereby catalyze a transformation in the culture.  But experiencing ego dissolution is one thing; integrating it into ordinary life is another. And, as the misadventures of the 1960s attest, building a culture around self-transcendence is a perilous (if inspiring) endeavor.  “Unraveling the Dream,” a new film presented by the Waking Up meditation app, explores whether the new science of psychedelics might shed fresh light on Huxley’s vision. Featuring original interviews with Anil Seth, Robin Carhart-Harris, and Shamil Chandaria, the film takes viewers on a sweeping journey to the frontiers of neuroscience and through the rich, turbulent history of psychedelics.

metas.name: Butt/Stuff
metas.description: Two anxious roommates fight over the most hallowed of queer spaces… the bathroom.

metas.name: Talon Mundo's Growing Up; Giving Up
metas.description: Dad's disagreement with his son causes a reminiscence of better times. As it has a greater effect on him than ever expected. Transcending and fracturing space and time as we know it. Forever altering their relationship as well the greater good of humanity. Determined to fix his bond with his son he must go through hell and back to achieve it. Along the way he faces his greatest challenges yet that will push him to his absolute limits. Showing what it takes so he can rekindle that once great relationship with his son and saving humanity as we know it also to protect the world from an approaching threat of catastrophic proportions.

metas.name: You Say We Aren't Objects...
metas.description: A collection of anonymously shared stories detailing women’s experiences of sexualization, showcasing that it may not be ‘every man,’ but it is every woman.

metas.name: Malevolent
metas.description: In 2019 the term 'incelcore' was used to refer to a group of independent musicians whose music dealt with themes of isolation, alienation, and a general distaste for many aspects of modern society  In 2020, during the wake of the pandemic, the scene grew substantially and in 2021 their first live festival, 'virginfest', was held for the scene.  The following footage was filmed between 2024-2025 from New York to Texas.

metas.name: KillTonyMania
metas.description: Kill Tony tag teams with WrestleMania 42, as Tony Hinchcliffe, WWE icons and comedy heavyweights put upstart stand-ups to the test in Las Vegas.

metas.name: Rogues Unscripted
metas.description: Introducing the Company of Rogues Actors’ Studio, composed of talking heads, this short documentary is just a sneak peak to understand what it means to be brave and take your first step to understand the work, industry, and craft. To chase the dream. To make it your own.

metas.name: axel's portal
metas.description: A performance art piece by the singer yeule. Eighteen of the 'Skullcrusher' prints are structurally placed in a grid pattern, and painted over in black acrylic paint, and sealed with candle wax.

metas.name: Aint no saints in queens
metas.description: The film sees a business deal between two notorious crime families unravel. (Film can be found on Sethlikesmovies YouTube channel)

metas.name: The Devil Wears Prada 2 Red Carpet World Premiere
metas.description: Get ready for couture, cameras, and cast moments as Disney+ and Hulu livestream the The Devil Wears Prada 2 world premiere from New York City’s Lincoln Center. Streaming live to subscribers worldwide, the event offers a rare, front‑row view as it unfolds in America’s fashion capital. From red carpet arrivals to behind‑the‑scenes interviews, it’s an inside look at one of the most glamorous nights of the year, and the perfect kickoff to the film’s theatrical debut.

metas.name: Protagonist
metas.description: A woman on the verge of emotional collapse is replaced by a more efficient version of herself.
A woman recounts her experience with aliens.

metas.name: Mr. Fritz
metas.description: 'Meet the lovable Mr. Fritz! He's the one, the only, star of the show. Forever...'  A lovable TV icon is forced to perform the same show again and again at the command of his production company. Seeking a way out, Mr. Fritz pitches an idea for a new show!

metas.name: GRRRL STYLE YYC
metas.description: GRRRL STYLE YYC is a collage of the femme punk scene in Calgary. Experience the scene like never before, through the eyes of the young girls that push it forward.

metas.name: How I Got My Wings
metas.description: After an unsettling encounter with a religious man, a missionary's perspective on his faith is skewed and polluted by powerful encounters with evil omens.

metas.name: Find Your Pack
metas.description: Explore the lived experiences of queer players within roller derby, showcasing the sport’s unique commitment to inclusivity and community.

metas.name: Elevated
metas.description: Have you ever had a dream that, that you, um, you had, your, you, you could, you'll do, you, you wants, you, you could do so, you, you'll do, you could, you, you want, you want them to do you so much you could do anything?

metas.name: Tuesday Women
metas.description: An idyllic morning: spaghetti bubbles on the stove, classical music plays. The calm is broken by a call that sets into motion a surreal day in which the phones keep ringing and mysterious, fascinating women interrupt his domestic routine, to provide strangely profound insights into his life. Three short stories by Haruki Murakami adapted to an Indian setting.

metas.name: Chasing A High
metas.description: Plot TBD

metas.name: I Don't Speak Dog (But I'll Try)
metas.description: A filmmaker unpacks her evolving relationship with her adolescent German Shepherd, confronting the painful question of why, despite raising him, he seems to love her partner more.

metas.name: Amma, Do Giraffes Cry?
metas.description: A whimsical filmmaker turns to his rational zoologist mother to help film animals. As memories of tigers and pet rabbits surface, the zoologist is compelled to question her relationship with animals.

metas.name: Downtown Loft with Skylights
metas.description: In this late work (made with Azazel Jacobs’s participation), Ken Jacobs returns to an earlier (and frequent) subject, the bric-a-brac-laden loft in which he and Flo lived, and from it he derives a lacunar, fragmented, sculptural reenvisioning of on-screen (and domestic) space. Made in 2024, world premiere at Film at Lincoln Center on April 20, 2026.

metas.name: I Love You, Jocelyn
metas.description: I Love You Jocelyn is an action packed, funny, feel good short that follows Jo, a spirited girl living in a tropical cursed paradise. Can she use her magic powers to save the day?

metas.name: KLS
metas.description: A teenager suffering from KLS (Kleine-Levin Syndrome), also known as 'Sleeping Beauty Syndrome' awakes from a horrifying dream.

metas.name: Restoring the Headwaters
metas.description: The Headcut in the Headwaters follows the story of wildlife biologists and stream ecologists as they take on the urgent challenge of repairing a stream headcut in the fragile sage-steppe ecosystem of the Big Hole Valley in Montana. What begins as an eroding scar in the landscape becomes a threat to drain wetlands, degrade critical sage-grouse habitat and disrupt water flowing downstream. Through collaboration, grit, and innovative restoration, this short documentary reveals how working lands and wildlife can thrive together and why protecting water at its source is essential for the resilience of rivers, communities, and all ecosystems downstream.

metas.name: Spillover – Planet of Viruses
metas.description: Ebola, Marburg, Nipah, COVID-19, and now avian flu – a long list of deadly viruses leaping from animals to humans. Why are these spillovers happening, and how are we fuelling them? Directed by award-winner Michael Wech and produced by Emmy®-winner Leopold Hoesch, this film takes us on a gripping global journey into the origins of some of the world’s most dangerous pathogens. For anyone who wants to understand the science behind pandemics and how we hopefully can prevent the next one.

metas.name: Monachopsis
metas.description: The subtle but persistent feeling of constantly being out of place.

metas.name: To The Ones We've Loved Forever
metas.description: Through an old photobook, we reminisce the memories of the ones we’ve loved forever.

metas.name: a voyage of eulogies
metas.description: In the near future, a filmmaker documents the last traces of life on Planet Earth. As his journey unfolds, fragments of the past resurface in conversation, giving way to invisible histories that begin to speak and unseen presences that emerge, rooted in the memory of a long-lost loved one. Through the act of capturing and recording, he attempts to grasp loss and impermanence.

metas.name: Juvenile Inspektor: The Shadow Over Jõhvi
metas.description: In Soviet-occupied Estonia, a boy is caught between a broken family, the suffocating grip of state power, and forces far older than the empire itself. As propaganda and betrayal close in, he discovers that the greatest danger emerges where political oppression and ancient myth converge - and that his own bloodline may hold the key to both survival and ruin.

metas.name: CTRL+Z Stuck in the Edit
metas.description: In the world of film theory, the director is the supreme creator. However, for Bob, the protagonist of this one-minute meta-metaphysical nightmare, that hierarchy collapses with a single keystroke. While trying to enjoy a well-deserved moment of peace with a piece of cake, Bob realizes his reality doesn't belong to him, but to an invisible Editor. Time for Bob ceases to be linear-he becomes a victim of a brutal "Undo" process. Through a series of violent jump cuts, absurd wardrobe changes, and forced rewinds, Bob experiences a digital version of purgatory. The film explores the thin line between the silver screen and reality, turning a technical glitch into existential horror. In the end, Bob doesn't seek salvation from a director; instead, he attempts to "cut" the very light source of the machine that created him, proving that in the digital age, the only true God is the one with their finger on CTRL+Z.

metas.name: As Rare As I Am
metas.description: AS RARE AS I AM is an intimate portrait of Allyx Theus, whose lifelong love of climbing is tested by the biases and barriers women face in the sport. From the moment she first stepped into a climbing gym, Allyx noticed she was treated differently, and early on, she believed skill and strength would earn her respect. But even as she grew stronger and mastered harder routes, a new challenge emerged: reconciling her muscular body with society’s expectations of what a woman “should” look like. The film follows Allyx as she learns to climb for herself, embracing the joy of the sport rather than the approval of others. Now, as Director of her own gym, she works to create the inclusive, supportive community she never had.

metas.name: Some Girl
metas.description: A young woman gets away for a romantic weekend with her new boyfriend and is eager to win over his friends. Some Girl is a film about hidden violence — not only the harm done to one person, but the impact on everyone who chooses not to look too closely.

metas.name: The Big Brothers S1E5: Corporate Outlaw
metas.description: A businessman networks with two outlaws mid-duel.

metas.name: Seven Till Heaven
metas.description: After a teenager has reached his limit coping with terminal illness, he chooses to stop taking his medication and enjoy his last week on Earth the best he can.

metas.name: The Bite
metas.description: When an anxious model believes he's been bit on the forearm, he will set out to find his assailant in order to confirm his fear.

metas.name: There is a Dead Body in Phillip Daniels' Apartment
metas.description: Upon discovery of a mysterious dead body in his apartment, Phillip Daniels resolves to not let such a superfluous thing ruin his fun night, much to the chagrin of his friend, Jeffrey Walker.

metas.name: Waster
metas.description: A guy called Tim Redford has to battle his way through trial and tribulation whilst preparing for his debut TV Appearance as a musician on a Talent Show.

metas.name: Dream
metas.description: In the bad dreams of a guy with social anxiety and ornithophobia...

metas.name: The Key to Success
metas.description: What's up everypony!  A group of college students get locked in a study room while some friends go on an adventure to find the right door.

metas.name: Queen Elizabeth II: Her Story, Our Century
metas.description: Marking what would have been Queen Elizabeth II’s 100th year, this landmark documentary tells the story of her life alongside a century of momentous change in Britain. Through archive and eyewitness reflections, the film explores how she led the monarchy through profound social, political and cultural change. Contributions from some of Britain’s best-known faces and international figures, including Dame Helen Mirren, Sir David Attenborough, Barack Obama and Queen Camilla, reveal how she remained a constant in a changing world.

metas.name: Ideal Candidate
metas.description: Ainsley Bruce attempts to get his life back on track in the form of a job interview. All seems well on the surface, However his demons from his past continue to haunt him.

metas.name: LIVE matches from WWE World: April 19, 2026
metas.description: A broadcast from WWE(World Wrestling Entertainment) of the Live Matches from WWE World it is the second of two broadcast of the same name on the same day.

metas.name: Spark Joshi: Lady Luck II
metas.description: Watch Spark Joshi: Lady Luck II live on TrillerTV-straight from Las Vegas!  Experience the unmatched athleticism and charisma of Japanese women's pro wrestling. With two world championships on the line and a roster of certified legends, Lady Luck II is the definitive Joshi event of the 2026 Las Vegas season.

metas.name: LIVE matches from WWE World: April 19, 2026
metas.description: A broadcast from WWE(World Wrestling Entertainment) of the Live Matches from WWE World it is the first of two broadcast of the same name on the same day.

metas.name: Arsenal Vs Man City
metas.description: The Big Match

metas.name: Crossed Paths
metas.description: Two exes unexpectedly reunite, but they have very different ideas on what they want their relationship with each other to be.

metas.name: Billy Luck
metas.description: “First I was his documentarian, then I was his enabler, now I’m his doppelganger...” Over 25 years ago, filmmaker Jeff Krulik fell under the spell of local photographer, musician and writer Chris Earnshaw, a manic yet fascinating walking encyclopedia of Washington, DC’s cultural and architectural history. The two started wandering the streets of DC, recording Chris riffing on the vanishing cityscape — the lost downtown he captured in photos beginning as a child, eventually amassing thousands of stills. But as the hours of footage and the years wore on, Chris found himself a local art star, with a front-page story in the Washington Post Arts section, while Jeff’s original documentary endeavor turned upside down, going haywire, becoming something else entirely. A film by Jeff Krulik, Greg DeLiso and Dina Selfridge.

metas.name: Bad Decisions (And Why They Matter)
metas.description: In an attempt to escape the mundane, a group of friends try something that's a little more on the dangerous side.

metas.name: Cleanup on Aisle Adorno
metas.description: The Culture Industries go grocery shopping in this found footage bleach and color over black and white super 8.

metas.name: BETTER LIGHTING
metas.description: An agitprop made from two 30-second GE adverts from the 1970's repurposed to rebuke the role white women voters in the US have chosen to uphold white supremacy and the patriarchy instead of supporting the interests of women and anyone who loves them.

metas.name: Spirit Away
metas.description: An unexpected and mysterious encounter with a group of students from Japan on a beach in Hawaii. Inspired by the work and life of Ryuichi Sakamoto (1952-2023). Sound design includes motifs and themes from his music.

metas.name: Prefer Not To Say
metas.description: In an age dominated by identity politics and increasingly intrusive bureaucracies, this work is a celebration of privacy, personal freedom, and diversity of being. As we are defined and divided by institutional language and algorithmic data systems, urgent global issues affecting all of humanity are spiraling out of control and being ignored. Choosing to Prefer Not to Say is an act of protest in a refusal to be reduced by bureaucratic categories that divide and flatten the complexity of our lived experience.

metas.name: Death in the Archive
metas.description: Death in the Archive is an inquiry into forgotten films and artists’ lives, tracing a path from personal to institutional archives. The film unfolds as a historical montage and diaristic mapping of visionary Beat filmmakers of San Francisco’s North Beach, conjuring the celluloid bodies and creative metabolisms that persist amid the human and material mortality of artists, images, and fragile film stocks. Fragments of Dion Vigné, Jordan Belson, and Christopher MacLaine intertwine with personal histories and cinematic visions, constructing an archaeology of memory. Preservation of both media and life becomes an ephemeral transmission — the fragile survival of memory. Gestures, voices, and images endure, transcending time and material limits, as the film bears witness to absence, endurance, and the persistent life of creative imagination.

metas.name: Liberty
metas.description: Liberty looks within the safe space of a relationship and how culture can be held creatively or destructively within the body.

metas.name: here and there, now and then
metas.description: Two dancers move fluidly and visually through space and time, their movements uniting historical black and white film footage with contemporary colour video.

metas.name: APE
metas.description: A comedic dance film exploration of our evolution, and the human struggle to connect with each other in an increasingly digital landscape, and as we grow increasingly more addicted to our phones and devices.

metas.name: body landscapes
metas.description: Self-portraits of the artist’s emotional states to process the experience of going through breast cancer during the pandemic.

metas.name: the window kind of opens by itself
metas.description: The window kind of opens by Itself takes place at the intergenerational, embodied intersection of four important experimental dance artists. It explores radical expressions of self within duet form to reveal intimate and epic portals of connection, divergence, and aliveness.

metas.name: Dancing with Moloch
metas.description: This performance mixes B movies, ecstatic dancers, animations, live and recorded poetry, personal elegies, remembrances including an auto-eulogy, zombies, Allen Ginsberg, John Denver, Black Sabbath, Charles Manson, Wheel of Fortune, Buddy Rich, Jelly Roll Morton, Max Fleischer, Donald O’Finn, Feedbuck, Akio Mokuno, Donald Miller, Emmalee Sutton, to name but a few. 99’s multimedia Danse Macabre is joyous, funny, terrifying, thoughtful and playful.

metas.name: Murder at the Merriweather
metas.description: When her best friend falls to her death at the luxury resort where they both work, Sophie fights to learn the truth of what happened. But in this world of haves and have-nots, everyone hides a secret.

metas.name: BE HAPPIER
metas.description: Shot on Super 8 at a framerate equivalent to the beats of a hummingbird’s wing, the artist documents a buoyant open-air conversation with her 103-year-old grandmother in a garden.

metas.name: Dreams Travel Wondrous Wires
metas.description: Imagine a dream-centric world. A world more focused on inner than outer. A world where reality begins in dreams. The writer Jane Roberts dreamt of such a world. In the 1970s-80s, she published a series of best-selling books collectively known as the “Seth Material”. Drawn from these texts, and filmed in the house where she lived and wrote, this is a nocturnal meditation, an oneiric exercise, a dream seed, a primer for the movie your mind will make when you go to sleep tonight.

metas.name: Semaphore to the Sea
metas.description: The artist uses semaphore—a maritime-based communication system that uses handheld flags to convey information at a distance—to transmit a message to the sea. Created at the end of an artist residency at the Sou’wester in Seaview, WA.

metas.name: Remembering Tornado Season
metas.description: A visual poem reflecting on Missouri’s tornado season and the destruction left in its wake.

metas.name: Gaseous Beings
metas.description: This triptych plays on polysemy and repetition to simultaneously address the encounters that unsettle us, impact us, and change us, as well as the chemical exchanges that take place in a polluted environment.

metas.name: The Disastro Theorem
metas.description: Stay-at-home mad scientist Dr. Disastro and his loyal lab assistant Igor try to hatch a scheme to take over the world before the end of the week. Can they be stopped?

metas.name: Cities of Ghosts and Angels
metas.description: In Los Angeles and Tokyo, the film invites people to live out unfulfilled desires as strangers from their city embody those they long for. The film explores loneliness and emotional life in late-capitalist cities, all while celebrating the transformative power of imagination.

metas.name: Clunk Burger
metas.description: Crude comics, bad drawings, and absurd poetry come to life as an animated, adult picture book.

metas.name: Never Seen
metas.description: Two friends enter an abandoned house looking for vintage tech and discover something inside that has been waiting for them.

metas.name: Edinburgh '25
metas.description: Orla revisits her happiest memory of 2025.

metas.name: Finger Guns
metas.description: A privileged office worker rebels when his freedom to use the "finger gun" gesture is threatened in this comedic satire.

metas.name: How It Is
metas.description: This play is adapted from the last full-length novel by Samuel Beckett, also one of his most obscure works.Set in a boundless wilderness, the story follows an unnamed narrator crawling forward. On his journey, he encounters another wanderer named Pim, and an absurd yet tense relationship gradually develops between the two.In the end, Pim chooses to flee, leaving the narrator alone once again.

metas.name: Exploring Our Place
metas.description: Exploring Our Place is filmed and narrated by a group of children from Northwood Primary School in Thamesmead.

metas.name: Square
metas.description: Women’s struggle unfolds against London as both container and witness.

metas.name: Stories from Co-op Pepys
metas.description: A conversation with Daniel, a member of Co-o Pepys Arts Project which was established by residents of the Pepys estate in 1981. He talks about his memories and experiences of living on the estate for more than two decades, accompanied by footage of the Arts Project workspaces.

metas.name: The Dreamers
